message:
refactor(api): replace OnceLock with atomics for lock-free process start time tracking

The previous implementation used `OnceLock<ProcessStartState>` to pin
process start time, which meant the first read permanently locked the
baseline even if it occurred before service bootstrap. This caused
incorrect uptime values when early health probes raced the init path.

Changes:
- Replace `OnceLock<ProcessStartState>` with a `LazyLock<Instant>`
  monotonic epoch plus `AtomicBool` / `AtomicU64` / `RwLock<String>`
  for lock-free read access to process start offset and wall-clock time
- Add `install_process_start()` that explicitly stores the start offset
  and wall-clock, so reads before `init_process_start()` return zero
  uptime instead of pinning a stale baseline
- Guard `process_uptime_seconds()` and `process_started_at_rfc3339()`
  with `PROCESS_START_INITIALIZED` acquire fence so pre-init reads are
  safe and return sensible defaults
- Import reformat in service.rs (rustfmt)

This ensures operator /health and /status endpoints report correct
uptime regardless of when they are first polled relative to bootstrap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
